channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Jimmy Carter's funeral begins by tracing 100 years from rural Georgia to the world stage
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter 's extended public farewell began Saturday in Georgia, with the 39th U.S. president’s flag-draped casket tracing his long arc from the Depression-era South and family farming business to the pinnacle of American political power and decades as a global humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JEFF AMY and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chicago Bulls plan to retire Derrick Rose's jersey next season
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Derrick Rose wasn't sure how he will feel when sees his No. 1 hanging from the rafters. He was still trying to process the news. The Chicago Bulls announced Saturday they plan to retire Rose's jersey sometime next season.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Drivers skid and crash as wintry mix grips central US before moving east
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — Road conditions were deteriorating Saturday in the central U.S. as a winter storm brought a mix of snow, ice and plunging temperatures, with forecasts calling for the dreaded combo to spread eastward in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington Post cartoonist quits after paper rejects sketch of Bezos bowing to Trump
item.description: A Washington Post cartoonist says she has decided to quit after editors rejected her sketch of the newspaper's owner and other media executives bowing to President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hillary Clinton, George Soros and Denzel Washington will receive the highest US civilian honor
item.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, Democratic philanthropist George Soros and actor-director Denzel Washington will be awarded the nation's highest civilian honor on Saturday in a White House ceremony.
item.dc:creator: KREX 5

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: A U.S. District Judge denied a Jan. 6 rioter’s request to attend President-elect Trump's inauguration later this month, citing concerns over the Capitol insurrection which incited hours of chaos and violence in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Filmmaker and husband to Aubrey Plaza, Jeff Baena, dies by suicide: report
item.description: Film writer and director Jeff Baena, who is also known for his marriage to Hollywood actress Aubrey Plaza, has died at the age of 47, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German leader is more worried about Musk's backing of a far-right party than his insults
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German Chancellor Olaf Scholz says he's staying “cool” against critical personal comments made by Elon Musk but finds it worrying that the U.S. billionaire makes the effort to get involved in a general election by endorsing the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) party.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: IRS reminds taxpayers in 24 disaster area states of 2025 deadlines
item.description: A tax filing extension for residents of two dozen states affected by disasters is coming to an end in 2025, the IRS reminded taxpayers Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, WGN 9

item.title: Suspected illegal immigrant's fake bomb threat shuts down SC highway
item.description: A suspected illegal immigrant was arrested in South Carolina after officials say he made a fake bomb threat during a traffic stop which ultimately led to a section of the I-85 being shut down for nearly five hours.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Soldier who died by suicide in Las Vegas told ex-girlfriend of pain and exhaustion after Afghanistan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The highly decorated Special Forces soldier who died by suicide in a Cybertruck explosion on New Year's Day confided to a former girlfriend who had served as an Army nurse that he faced significant pain and exhaustion that she says were key symptoms of traumatic brain injury.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mother, son given lengthy prison terms for roles in the killings of 8 Ohio family members
item.description: Two more family members convicted in the killings of eight members of an Ohio family received lengthy prison terms on Friday for their roles in the 2016 shootings, as prosecutions near completion in what has been described as the most heinous crime in modern Ohio history.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Craziest air travel moments of 2024
item.description: The year 2024 was filled with numerous incidents aboard airplanes from crashes and fights to liquids making their way from the bathroom through the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LeBron James breaks Michael Jordan's record for 30-point games with his 563rd
item.description: LeBron James broke Michael Jordan's NBA record for 30-point games during the Los Angeles Lakers' victory over Atlanta on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2nd Homeland Security agent in Utah arrested on charges of selling illicit drugs that were seized as evidence
item.description: A second Department of Homeland Security agent in Utah was arrested after allegedly using a confidential informant to sell illicit drugs from evidence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know as snow, freezing rain and bitter cold heads through much of the US
item.description: A major winter storm began Saturday in the central U.S. and is forecast to move east over the next several days.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Assumption University students charged in 'To Catch a Predator' TikTok scheme
item.description: Five Assumption College students are facing charges after allegedly luring a man to campus, framing him as a sexual predator and then attacking him, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: Soldier who blew up Tesla at Trump hotel left note saying blast was to be a 'wake up call' for US
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A highly decorated Army soldier who fatally shot himself in a Tesla Cybertruck just before it blew up outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas left notes saying the New Year's Day explosion was a stunt to serve as a “wake up call” for the country’s ills, investigators said Friday.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, TARA COPP, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Blinken heads to Asia, Europe on last expected trip as top US diplomat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken will embark on what is expected to be his final overseas trip in office this weekend, traveling to South Korea, Japan and France. The State Department announced Friday that Blinken would visit Seoul, Tokyo and Paris beginning Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Altos woman who plotted to kill estranged husband makes bid for parole
item.description: (KRON) -- A Los Altos woman who attempted to kill her estranged husband while the couple was going through a bitter divorce will appear in front of a state parole board Friday asking for freedom.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: University of Michigan lab develops blood test to identify HPV-related cancers
item.description: Researchers at a University of Michigan lab have developed a “first-of-its-kind” blood test to detect head and neck cancers caused by human papillomavirus.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but promised not to jail him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Ex-employee charged after babies found with 'unexplainable fractures' at Virginia hospital
item.description: The charges comes after the hospital said three babies were discovered with "unexplainable fractures" in late November and December of 2024. A similar incident took place the previous year.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, Fox 5

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star Zachery Ty Bryan jailed
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan was arrested this week on a domestic violence charge in South Carolina, online jail records show.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, KRON4

item.title: FBI releases new video of suspect planting pipe bomb on eve of Capitol riot
item.description: A harrowing chapter in American history remains shrouded in mystery: Who planted pipe bombs outside offices of the Democratic and Republican national committees in Washington on the eve of the attack on the Capitol?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: How to adjust your budget if you're unemployed
item.description: More than a quarter (26%) of households live "paycheck to paycheck," spending over 95% of their income on necessities.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: Biden awards the 2nd highest civilian award to leaders of the Jan. 6 committee and 18 others
item.description: President Joe Biden on Thursday awarded the second highest civilian medal to Liz Cheney and Bennie Thompson, leaders of the congressional investigation into the Capitol riot who Donald Trump has said should be jailed for their roles in the inquiry.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cybertruck explosion marks second high-profile incident at Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: The Cybertruck fire and explosion was preceded by a 2021 bomb threat that was meant to serve as a message to Trump.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, Fox 5

item.title: Man pushed onto NYC subway tracks recovering while suspect is charged with attempted murder
item.description: A man survived being shoved onto subway tracks ahead of an incoming train in New York City on New Year's Eve and is expected to fully recover, relatives said, while the person accused of pushing him was being held without bail Thursday on attempted murder and assault charges.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Deputies are stuck using radios with Los Angeles County's dispatch system out of commission
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The archaic computer dispatch system for the nation's largest sheriff's department remained out of commission on Thursday after crashing on New Year's Eve, forcing deputies to handle all calls by radio, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: John Capodice, actor who appeared in 'Ace Ventura,' 'Seinfeld' and more, dies at 83
item.description: News of Capodice’s death on Monday was publicly announced in an obituary posted by the Pizzi Funeral Home in Northvale, New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: FBI found 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home during search in December, prosecutors say
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Federal agents found one of the largest stockpiles of homemade explosives they have ever seized when they arrested a Virginia man on a firearms charge last month, according to a court filing by federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cruise ship stomach bug illnesses hit 12-year high: CDC
item.description: Cruise ships saw the highest number of stomach bug illnesses since 2012 with 16 outbreaks impacting a total of 1,894 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, Fox 5

item.title: NYPD presence increased at Trump Tower, Times Square after attacks in New Orleans, Las Vegas
item.description: “While there are no immediate threats to our city at this time, out of an abundance of caution, we have heightened security and have increased NYPD presence at relevant locations"
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: New Orleans attack victims: What we know so far
item.description: Investigations continue into an "act of terrorism" in New Orleans' bustling Canal and Bourbon streets after a truck drove into a crowd, killing 15 people and injuring at least 35 more.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WGN 9

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed to Thursday night
item.description: Hours following the New Year's Day tragedy on Bourbon Street, Wednesday's Allstate Sugar Bowl bout between Notre Dame and Georgia is being postponed.
item.dc:creator: Richie Mills, ABC 4
